title: ‘There’s nothing compared to that feeling’: Red Sox players discussed return from World Baseball Classic
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Red Sox players returned to spring training after participating in the World Baseball Classic, where Venezuela won its first title.
Red Sox's Ranger Suarez pitched for Venezuela, facing crowds of 30-35,000 at the tournament compared to 13 people in practice.
Willson Contreras, also on Venezuela's roster, valued the WBC over the World Series, emphasizing national pride and team chemistry.

title: Marlon Wayans stopped by this South Boston restaurant for lunch
author: John Waller
bullets: Marlon Wayans visited Hunter's Kitchen & Bar in South Boston for lunch during his 'Wild Child Tour'.
Wayans praised the southern food at the restaurant, sharing a photo on Instagram with chef Lambert Givens.
The comedian is performing in Worcester on Thursday and at the Zeiterion in New Bedford on Friday.

title: Nine Winters Bakery is closing in Cambridge: ‘I’m choosing my family’
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Nine Winters Bakery in Cambridge is closing on March 29, citing owner Marissa Ferola's decision to focus on family.
The bakery, recognized for its unique Korean-American treats, operated for less than a year at its permanent location.
Nine Winters began as a pop-up and was known for dishes like Gochujang sticky buns and kimchi cheese danishes.

title: Stephen Curry swung by this North End restaurant while in town for Celtics-Warriors
author: John Waller
bullets: Stephen Curry visited Strega in Boston's North End while in town for a game against the Celtics.
Strega's Instagram celebrated Curry's visit, describing their locale as 'where championship energy meets Italian indulgence.'
Curry, recovering from a knee injury, was in Boston as his team faced the Celtics, who won 120-99.

title: It’s finally payday for former Boston Sports Clubs members
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts AG's Office to distribute $4 million to 47,000 former Boston Sports Clubs members wrongly charged during pandemic shutdowns.
Court ruled former CEO Patrick Walsh unlawfully billed members and violated Health Club Act by hindering contract cancellations.
Refunds, about $85 per member, follow Boston Sports Club's bankruptcy and rebranding as New York Sports Club under Town Sports International.

title: Beat out by a hot tamale: Maine hosts the second-best specialty food festival in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Maine Whoopie Pie Festival in Dover-Foxcroft ranked No. 2 in USA Today's 2026 10Best specialty food festivals.
The Delta Hot Tamale Festival in Greenville, Mississippi, claimed the top spot in USA Today's specialty food festival rankings.
The Maine festival features bakers competing in whoopie pie variations, eating contests, and family-friendly activities on June 13.

title: Man accused of recording people in South Shore library bathroom
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Police arrested Thomas Campbell, 63, for allegedly recording people in a restroom at Cohasset's Paul Pratt Public Library.
Library staff found and reported a hidden device, prompting a police investigation that confirmed illegal recordings.
Campbell faces charges of illegal wiretapping and video recording and is held on $2,500 bail, with arraignment set for Friday.

title: Energy assistance runs out amid spiking oil prices
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Fuel assistance funds in Massachusetts are depleting amid a 30% spike in heating oil prices due to the Iran conflict.
Action for Boston Community Development reports over 70% of 170,000 households have exhausted their fuel assistance benefits.
The Healey administration raised fuel assistance to $1,400 as oil prices surged, but many families still face heating challenges.

title: Investing in quality: The benefits of well-made furniture and art
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Interior designer Kate Maloney uses locally made furniture for a Cambridge home, emphasizing quality and craftsmanship.
Seven hand-blown glass lighting fixtures by Tracy Glover Studio inspired the color scheme for the Cambridge kitchen.
Repurposing quality furniture and drapes exemplifies Maloney's sustainable design approach, saving costs over replacing with new items.

title: ‘He drives them’: New Bruins forward Lukas Reichel made an instant impact at TD Garden
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel scored a goal and an assist in his Bruins debut, leading a 6-1 win over the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel was acquired from the Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round pick and called up after three games in Providence.
The Bruins are tied in points with the Red Wings and Canadiens, holding the first wild-card spot in the Eastern Conference.
